<<Course Structure and How to Get the Most Out of It>>

*highlighted section*  
**1. Understand the Course Layout**  
Before diving into the content, take time to map out the course structure. Most courses have a clear outline with modules, weekly topics, and assessments. For example, [module 1] might focus on [topic A], while [module 2] covers [topic B]. Knowing where you are in the course helps you stay on track and avoid confusion. Always check the [syllabus] for deadlines and expectations.

*highlighted section*  
**2. Prioritize Active Learning**  
A minimalist approach means focusing on what truly matters. Instead of passively reading, engage with the material through [practice exercises], [discussion forums], and [self-assessment quizzes]. For instance, after [lesson 3], apply your knowledge by completing [assignment 1]. This builds retention and prepares you for [final project].

*highlighted section*  
**3. Set Personal Goals and Track Progress**  
Set small, achievable goals for each [week] or [module]. For example, aim to complete [task X] by [date] or understand [concept Y] by [timeframe]. Use tools like [calendar] or [note-taking app] to track your progress. Reflect on what you’ve learned after [each module] to ensure you’re getting the most out of [course name].
